title: Calymmodon denticulatus
taxonomy: Calymmodon denticulatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, which belongs to the order Polypodiales. It is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Calymmodon. The Polypodiaceae family is a group of vascular plants that are primarily epiphytes or terrestrial. The Polypodiales order is a large and diverse group of ferns, including over 1,200 species. Calymmodon denticulatus is a tropical plant native to the Pacific island regions.

intro: Calymmodon denticulatus, also known as the toothed Calymmodon, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Calymmodon denticulatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calymmodon denticulatus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and flu.
